1.2: FRISSON is a soulful living hospitality
1.3: Architects · Sound Designer · Interior Designer · Biologist · HCI Researcher · Material Artist · Writers · Athletes · Frontier AI Researcher · Astronauts · Medical Engineers · Marine Scientist
1.4: We are redesigning the best hospitality in the world
1.5: In the Moon / Mars / And Beyond...
1.6: (nav) More-Than-Human Cities / Sona AI / Advocater Program
2.1:
A world of your own to forge.
What will it become?
2.5: "Out of avidity to see the Immortals, to touch that more-than-human City, I could hardly sleep. And as though the Troglodytes could divine my goal, they did not sleep either."
2.8: Collecting the Dreams of the Whole Earth
2.9: No city was ever made great by amassing the most stone. A city becomes great only when beauty — after five hundred years of wandering — chooses it, and there begins again, for the most creative souls among us.
2.10: We believe that beauty, in itself, has the power to migrate.
2.11: It passes through geographies, through wars, through the turning of civilizations, and alights only in the rarest of moments. To the places where it once paused, we pay homage.
2.12: Florence was once its waystation — and so were the fields of Devonshire, the seventh hill of Lisbon, and the forests of the Île-de-France.
2.13: Into our work we graft the aesthetic bloodline of Europe's four great Renaissance houses — Medici, Cavendish, Palmela, Montmorency — and render it in a contemporary language of building.
2.14a: Florence is Brunelleschi's proportion, the blue-grey pietra serena, Raphael's frescoes, and Buontalenti's man-made grotto in the Boboli Gardens.
2.14b: Chatsworth is a book never finished — from William Kent's seats and Paxton's glasshouse to Lucian Freud's portraits.
2.14c: The Palmela Palace holds Anatole Calmels's herms, the illusions of trompe-l'œil, the Eastern motifs of chinoiserie, and the tender glaze of blue-and-white azulejos.
2.14d: Montmorency's Écouen is a fortress that breathes, gathering the finest hands of an entire age — Jean Bullant's loggia, Jean Goujon's sculpture, Léonard Limosin's Limoges enamel, and the ceramics of Bernard Palissy and Masséot Abaquesne.
2.15: Our ultimate aim is to rebuild something more enduring, and more precious — respect for the human being.
2.17: To let art leave the canvas — to enter architecture, enter light, enter the body, and at last enter the human heart.
2.18: We infuse the inspiration of contemporary artists into architectural detail, and carry it into the world through computational design and 3D-printed walls. In the work of Jesperish, for instance, a sensing of ancient-and-future worlds explores identity, emotion and systems of symbols; through symbolism he awakens intuition, asking how architecture, culture and environment shape the inner world of human beings.
2.20: The true "more" is not to surpass the human, but to understand the human more deeply — and to honour them.
2.21: Because it is a precise orchestration made for human perception.
2.22: What we rise above is a poverty of imagination — the kind that treats people as data, and homes as machines. The twentieth-century city fell for a single metaphor: the house as "a machine for living in." And so we were handed spaces efficient but cold, where a person became just another variable in a process. What we set out to build is different — a home that knows how to feel.
2.23: Sona / Frisson BIO-AI OS / The Heart-Pounding Magic of Tuning Your Body.
3.1: Sona / Frisson BIO-AI OS / The Heart-Pounding Magic of Tuning Your Body.
3.2: More-Than-Human City properties, powered by Sona.
3.3: Sona is a spatial intelligence attuned to what you feel. She plays light and sound, temperature and space as a hand moves across strings — and every shift she makes is an answer to the mood of your moment. She reads your weariness and your quiet delight, and the longing you have folded into silence and not yet noticed in yourself.
3.4: And the far shore this melody reaches for is exactly this: the frisson beyond words — that shiver down the spine as beauty, at its very utmost, brushes past.
3.6: "what is essential is invisible to the eye."
3.7: Built around the human, Sona understands how residents live and how their bodies feel — accumulating a lasting personal living-health asset, and acting on it with proactive insight and recommendations.
3.8: Frisson property's interior advances biological measurements to calibrate
3.13: Sona guides you to what's next
3.14: 130 / 168 hours (One week) — Average time we spend at home in life
3.15: 200 TOPS+ — local private AI compute power
3.16: Sona designs atmosphere and guides your hormone level with continuous experiments
3.17: Data Sovereign — What happens in Frisson Stays in Frisson
3.18: Through spatial computing and artificial intelligence, we seem able to find a graceful path — one that fuses beauty with function, art with the health of human dwelling, and the physical world with the world of the spirit.
3.19: This path may carry us into a new stage of the global village — until we are able to build gracious homes even in the most extreme environments, and upon the surface of Earth-like planets.
Arrival: You're still on your way home, and from your ring the OS already knows today's HRV and stress. Fresh air has ramped up early; the purifier has PM2.5 in single digits; the formaldehyde module works on. The house has cleaned itself. The door camera recognizes you—and the parrot on your shoulder—and the entry light turns 90° toward you and rises: 1800K, just enough to find the shoe cabinet. The panel shifts load from battery to off-peak power. Everything's ready.
Unwind: The living room sinks to amber. The heat pump warms the floor to 26°C, soft underfoot. ANC kills the street's low hum, the windows hold STC past 45, panels eat the reverb, speakers breathe ambient music into the quiet. A degree cooler than daytime—it saw you were restless and brought it down. Humidity at 50%. A thread of cedar from the diffuser; in the corner, a grow box glows, giving off oxygen and the feeling of something alive in the room.
Nourish: The oven has picked tonight's recipe from your ingredients; the camera above quietly logs what you eat. Preheated. The hood goes silent—ANC in the duct—so you hear only the food. Water's at temperature. After cooking, purification waits for the readings to drop; a touch of citrus covers what lingers. And the dishes aren't your problem.
Bathe: In the bathroom, the mirror wakes. Thirty seconds of facial blood-flow analysis logs a checkup. The toilet reads each use—glucose, protein, ketones, pH—and speaks only when something's wrong. The tub is filled to 39°C. Light is warm white; two hours from sleep, the blue is already gone.
Descent: The bedroom has guessed your rhythm. Glass blacks out, curtains close. Light fades amber to deep red to nothing—one last thread. The mattress cools to 19°C. The bladders read today's body and shift: you sat too long, so they ease the lumbar load and lift your feet. Lavender. Brown noise, barely there. If you wore the EEG buds, they wait for your alpha waves and press them down with phase-locked sound.
Repair: You may be deep asleep. Your space is not. CO₂ never tops 600; the cool mattress coaxes out growth hormone; you turn, and the bladders answer in 200 ms. Humidity 45%, ions scrubbing the air. The heat pump moves three degrees of heat per degree of power, spending the day's banked electricity—and topping up the car. In the dark, security keeps watch like a sentinel that never sleeps.
Dawn: Thirty minutes before the alarm, light climbs red, orange, warm white—a sunrise. The glass clears; daylight seeps in. The mattress warms to 22°C. Citrus and mint. Your chosen wake-up, or just birdsong. The laundry robot dried and folded last night's clothes. Cortisol rises on its curve. Waking feels like your own idea.
4.2: Where will you choose to devote your life?
4.3: Be the first to sleep inside the Magic Conch.
4.5: For its first edition, we are inviting a rare few to spend the first nights inside the Frisson Magic Conch — and to feel, first-hand, what a home can give back to a human life.
4.7: Help us shape a more-than-human city.
4.8: If you are — The Athlete / The Racing Driver / The Investor / The Singer / The Founder / The Artist
4.9: — and your life is spent in pursuit of what is right, we invite you to join the Advocater Program.
4.10: We are gathering a circle of advocaters to advise on the development of our More-Than-Human Cities properties, to refine how they are lived in, and to help decide what comes next.
4.12: In the early 20th century, the Wright brothers … history is written by heroic figures in pursuit of what is right.
4.14: We often think of our life's purpose as a mission. … but it's still a good life.
4.15: As you may already have realized, not all missions are created equal.
4.16: Put simply: a good adventure … or even makes it worse.
4.17: Today, we find ourselves in a crisis.
4.18: Silicon Valley's elite … and yet they go unaddressed.
4.19: In other words, if you are a person of extraordinary talent … it is a loss for humanity.
4.21: If reading this stirred something — come build it with us.

What happens in Frisson
Stays in Frisson
rec.cold.name: Cryotherapy Chamber
rec.cold.cap: Deep cellular renewal through liquid-nitrogen cold immersion — a surge of vitality that resets body and mind in moments.
rec.red.name: Infrared Sanctuary
rec.red.cap: Restorative warmth from full-spectrum infrared light, easing muscle and circulation as it reaches gently to the core.
rec.herb.name: Herbal Steam Ritual
rec.herb.cap: A Chinese medicinal steam ritual, where herb-laden vapour draws out fatigue and returns the body to balance.
rec.sky.name: Sky Pool
rec.sky.cap: Weightless calm in a pool suspended against the sky — where water and horizon dissolve into one.
rec.light.name: Sound & Light Therapy
rec.light.cap: Profound serenity through immersive Sound & Light Therapy — chromotherapy and healing frequencies woven into a tranquil setting, inspired by the visionary art of Alex Grey.
coll.lead: We build your climate-sealed castle in more-than-human beauty — at the world's most epic edge locations on Earth.
zahir.quote: "Zahir is the rose's shadow and the crack in the veil."
zahir.p1: Some things, once seen, can never be forgotten.
zahir.p2: Borges wrote of it as an ancient coin: whoever gazes upon it desires nothing else. The king, fearing that for its sake the world would forget the entire universe, had it sunk to the bottom of the sea.
zahir.p3: The coin is a mirror of free will and human nature.
zahir.p5: We reject one-dimensional living. From our very first building, we are forging a new global living network for the Digital Aristocracy — with Space, Art, and Soul as our mediums.
zahir.p6: Every residence becomes a Zahir that belongs to you — once it enters your life, it remains unforgettable.
zahir.climax: And all of this is for one purpose: to rebuild the Respect for Human.
zahir.features: 120 residences in Phase One · Spatial design that engages human emotion · The world's fastest elevator · Sona Bio OS · Soulful Living
zahir.spec.title: Key Features
zahir.spec.intro: The first Zahir — a residence engineered to engage the whole human, in body and in soul.
zahir.f5.v: Spatial design that engages human emotion
hiw.title: More than ownership
hiw.lead: More than ownership. A residence you hold, a network you inhabit, and an intelligence that travels with your body.
hiw.c1.title: One Key, Every Horizon
hiw.c1.body: Owning a single share unlocks an entire network of exceptional residences around the world — each one an equal to your own home. Through the Frisson app, reserve nights at your residence up to twelve months ahead, or trade them to stay at any other home in the collection, bookable up to four months out, with a modest adjustment where grades differ.
hiw.c2.title: Own a Fraction, Gain the Whole
hiw.c2.body: Each Frisson residence is offered in fractional ownership — the home divided into twelve shares, each granting thirty nights of stay a year. Hold as many shares as your life calls for. When you are away, Frisson can place your unused nights into its hospitality programme and return the corresponding fees to you. Because the architecture and craft hold their worth, a share is a lasting and appreciating asset — and after an initial period it may be released on a secondary market in ten-day increments.
hiw.c3.title: A Home That Remembers Your Body
hiw.c3.body: Through Sona, our Bio-AI OS, every residence keeps a long-term, private record of how you rest, breathe, and recover — an asset that is yours alone, whose key you alone hold. Arrive at any Frisson home in the world, and that key rebuilds your personal environment — light, sound, temperature, scent, and air — tuned to your body from the very first moment. Your health data never belongs to a place; it travels with you, and every place remembers you.
hiw.svc: No Maintenance · No Housekeeping · Private Concierge · Pay-Per-Use
hiw.close: Home is no longer a coordinate. It is a state that travels with you.
lv.title: Longevity Score
lv.lead: The way you eat, move and rest leaves a trace in every room. Radar and chemistry read it as your biological age — and the space works quietly and without pause to turn it younger.
lv.read.v: Ambient radar and chemistry sense how you live.
lv.reason.v: It traces the cause behind every shift.
lv.retune.v: The space adapts to what your body answers.
grotto.name: Zahir Sky Atelier
gallery.title: Space Healing your Body . Multisensory
grotto.tagline: Create what people truly want; listen to what technology makes possible.
grotto.vision:
Beauty once lingered in Florence for a full century; our ambition is to let it dwell again, the world over. This is how the Renaissance took the measure of the world: Brunelleschi's golden proportion, the blue-grey pietra serena, the five-centuries-unfaded colour Raphael sealed in buon fresco, and the axis in the Vasari Corridor that stitched power to beauty. And the deeper you go, the more beauty hides.

Through the layered green arcades of the Boboli Gardens you reach Buontalenti's man-made grotto — rustication dripping from the vault, walls inlaid with shell and tufa, water trembling across stone, statues half-revealed in an almost sfumato dusk.
deer.name: The Deer Garden
deer.vision:
In French Polynesia, emerald volcanic peaks rise straight from a turquoise lagoon. The serrated ridge of Mount Otemanu pierces the clouds; at its foot, layer upon layer of rainforest gives way to the most richly coloured lagoon on Earth — a dozen blues from pale green to deep sapphire, ringed like a natural rampart.

In the pastures of England the Cavendish family, across seventeen generations of patience, let their architecture grow out of the grass slopes — knowing the finest building does not conquer the terrain but follows its breath. In Polynesia we carry that growing onto the water: no longer pressing on the land, but floating, cantilevering, rising and falling with the lagoon's tide, like another coral grown from the sea.
hill.name: The Seventh Hill
hill.vision:
The Atacama — the driest place on Earth. Some reaches have gone centuries without rain; the ground so resembles Mars that NASA tests its rovers here, and ALMA, the world's largest telescope array, gazes into the cosmos above. This night sky is held to be the purest on the planet: air so thin it turns near-transparent, zero light pollution, the arch of the Milky Way close enough to touch.

The Palmela family once hid an interior of splendour behind a plain façade on Lisbon's seventh hill. True abundance never announces itself; it waits to be perceived. In the Atacama we carry that hidden abundance on — the shell sinking into the desert's ochre and silence, the interior a sanctuary thrown open to the universe.
fortress.name: The Breathing Fortress
fortress.vision:
Southeast Iceland, where the tongue of the Vatnajökull glacier melts into the sea, thousand-year ice drifts in bodies of blue and white across an ink-dark lagoon. Beside it lies Diamond Beach — shards of glacier polished by the surf into clear jewels, scattered over black volcanic sand. A land where ice meets fire, apocalyptic in its purity and solitude.

In the Île-de-France the Montmorency family dressed stone walls with the painted enamel of Limoges and cut loggias into thick ramparts to let light and wind pass through. The most fortified of things also knows the most tender way to open. In Iceland that paradox becomes the soul of the home — sealed against the Arctic cold, yet made to breathe, keeping its dweller in dialogue with the ice, the light, the polar night and endless day beyond.
gg.name: The Grotto Garden
gg.vision:
Jebel Shams, Oman's highest peak — the Mountain of the Sun. Below it plunges the kilometre-deep An Nakhur canyon, the Grand Canyon of Arabia: ochre-red walls falling away in tiers, ancient terraced villages clinging to the clefts, witness to a thousand years of patience and wisdom. This is the Gulf's most underrated epic ground — not the emptiness of desert, but the grandeur of living rock.

In the hills of Tuscany the Medici family quarried stone, raising walls and carving grottoes with the very warmth of the rock, letting architecture breathe with the mountain. That collaboration learned in stone — reading the character of the material, following the breath of the terrain — we learn anew on these Arabian cliffs. The architecture anchors into the canyon's strata, carved inward into a grotto, gazing out over a kilometre-deep abyss, the line between made and natural blurring here.
